title.en: Give me a break! 진짜 적당히 좀 해! 너 진짜 못 말린다!
title.ko: 아, 진짜 적당히 좀 해! 너 진짜 못 말린다!

description.en: When someone does something ridiculous or won't stop making corny jokes! Practice expressing your exasperation with 적당히 좀 해 and 못 말린다.
description.ko: 상대방이 너무 황당한 행동을 하거나 썰렁한 농담을 멈추지 않을 때! 적당히 좀 해와 못 말린다를 사용해 어이없는 마음을 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Exasperation. Annoyance. Friendship. Teasing. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I've decided to stop eating food. I'll just live on the love from our listeners starting today.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 지금 뭐라고 했어요? 사랑만 먹고 살겠다고요?
content.translation: John. what did you just say? You're going to live only on love?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yes. Love is the best energy source. I'll be fine!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜 적당히 좀 하세요. 배고프면 제일 먼저 간식 찾을 거면서 무슨 소리예요!
content.translation: Ah. seriously. give me a break. You'll be the first one looking for snacks when you're hungry!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 진짜 못 말린다. 그런 농담은 이제 그만하세요!
content.translation: You're really too much. Stop making those kinds of jokes!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 적당히 좀 해. This sounds like I'm asking someone to stop doing something too much. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 상대방이 지나친 장난을 치거나 귀찮게 할 때 쓰는 표현이에요.
content.translation: Yes. It's an expression used when someone plays excessive jokes or bothers you.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "이제 그만 좀 해" 라는 뜻을 아주 솔직하고 강하게 전달할 수 있죠.
content.translation: You can convey the meaning of "Stop it now" very honestly and strongly.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 적당히 좀 해.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 너 진짜 못 말린다.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 상대방의 고집이나 엉뚱한 행동을 아무도 막을 수 없다는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: This means that no one can stop the other person's stubbornness or eccentric behavior.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 황당하면서도 한편으로는 "그래. 너답다" 라며 포기할 때 자주 사용하는 리액션이에요.
content.translation: It's a reaction often used when you're exasperated but also giving up. thinking "Yeah. that's just like you."

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너 진짜 못 말린다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine I'm wearing sunglasses and a scarf even though we are indoors. because I want to look like a superstar.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 실내에서 선글라스는 왜 쓰고 있는 거예요? 심지어 스카프까지 하고 있네요!
content.translation: John. why are you wearing sunglasses indoors? You're even wearing a scarf!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Shh... I need to protect my privacy. Mina. My fans might find me here.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우리 지금 스튜디오 안에 있잖아요. 진짜 적당히 좀 하세요.
content.translation: John. we're inside the studio right now. Seriously. give me a break.

content.speaker: John
content.text: But the lighting here is so bright. I need to maintain my mysterious vibe.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜 못 말린다. 누가 보면 진짜 연예인인 줄 알겠어요!
content.translation: Wow. you're really too much. People would think you're an actual celebrity!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어서 선글라스 벗고 방송에 집중이나 하세요!
content.translation: Hurry up and take off your sunglasses and just focus on the show!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Sometimes friends need a reality check when they're being too extra.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 찐친이라면 가끔은 이렇게 따끔하고 솔직하게 리액션을 해주는 것도 필요하죠.
content.translation: Right. If you're best friends. it's sometimes necessary to give sharp and honest reactions like this.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘 배운 표현들로 친구의 엉뚱한 행동에 시원하게 한마디 해보세요!
content.translation: Say something refreshing to your friend's eccentric behavior with the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "exasperated" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 적당히 좀 해! Give me a break!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 너 진짜 못 말린다! You're too much!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 어이가 없네! This is ridiculous!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 황당하다. 정말! That's absurd. really!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 적당히 하세요! That's enough!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 친구가 너무 엉뚱한 소리를 한다면 당황하지 말고 말해 보세요. 적당히 좀 해!
content.translation: Everyone. if a friend says something too eccentric today. don't be flustered and try saying. "Give me a break!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 운동은 적당히 하는 것이 건강에 좋아요. (It's good for your health to exercise in moderation.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be absurd. To be ridiculous
vocabulary.example: 길을 가다가 돈을 줍다니 정말 황당한 일이에요. (It's really absurd to pick up money while walking on the street.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be dumbfounded. To be exasperated
vocabulary.example: 그의 거짓말을 들으니 정말 어이가 없네요. (I'm really dumbfounded to hear his lie.)
우리 아들의 장난은 정말 아무도 못 말려요. (No one can really stop my son's pranks.)
장난이 너무 지나치면 싸움이 될 수 있어요. (If a prank is too excessive. it can lead to a fight.)
그는 고집이 세서 남의 말을 잘 안 들어요. (He is stubborn and doesn't listen to others well.)
